info.title: TLS Protect Cloud API for Strata Cloud Manager
info.description: Use the TLS Protect Cloud APIs to manage certificates, certificate requests, applications, machine identities, users, teams, event logs, and more. This Open API spec file was created on April 28, 2026. © 2026 Palo Alto Networks, Inc. Palo Alto Networks is a registered trademark of Palo Alto Networks. A list of our trademarks can be found at https://www.paloaltonetworks.com/company/trademarks.html. All other marks mentioned herein may be trademarks of their respective companies.

get.description:
This endpoint allows you to retrieve all your certificates according to a specified criteria. This API provides a quick way to gather certificate details in order to install the certificates where they are needed.

Use the `subject` query parameter to limit the search based on the certificate subject common name.

parameters.description: Ability to enter regular expression to match specific criteria.
parameters.name: subject

parameters.description: Indicates the maximum integer number of certificates returned.
parameters.name: limit

200.description: Certificate(s) response.

post.description: Adds to the inventory one or more certificates to the inventory.the certificates are specified in raw base64-encoded format (PEM without header/footer). Chain CA certificates are optional but may be provided using `issuerCertificates`.

201.description: Certificates were imported; details in response body.

post.summary: Import a set of raw certificates

get.description: Retrieves the details of the certificate that has the specified `id`.

parameters.description: Indicates whether response should include nested ownership data (e.g., `owningContainers`).
parameters.name: ownershipTree

200.description: Certificate was found; details in response body.

get.description: Exports the certificate that has the specified `id` in PEM or DER format. Chain CA certificates are included for PEM format when `chainOrder` is EE_FIRST or ROOT_FIRST.

200.description: Raw certificate in response body

post.description: Submits one or more certificates for TLS validation.
post.summary: Request validation for a set of

post.description: This endpoint retires one or more certificates by using the **certificateIds** parameter to match a specified value.

200.description: Certificate retirement response.

post.summary: Retire certificates

post.description: Recover the certificates specified by `certificateIds`, including any previous versions of those certificates.

200.description: Certificates were recovered. Total number and details are in the response body.

post.summary: Recover a set of certificates

post.description: Permanently deletes the retired certificates specified by `certificateIds` from the inventory.
post.summary: Delete a set of retired certificates

post.description: This endpoint retrieves certificate data according to specified search criteria based on commonly used field search parameters. Some examples are `signatureHashAlgorithm`, `validityEnd`, and `issuerCN`. For more information, see [common search parameters](https://docs.venafi.cloud/api/about-api-search-fields/).

200.description: Certificate response.

post.summary: Retrieve certificate data matching search criteria

get.description:
Use this endpoint allows you to retrieve certificate instances according to specified criteria. Total certificate instances are the sum of the number of certificate installations, plus the total number of certificates that have no installations. Trusted CA certificates are not counted. For more information, see [certificate instances](https://docs.venafi.cloud/CSH_view_instances).

Use query parameters to limit your search based on certificate criteria.

200.description: Certificate response.

get.description: Retrieves the details of the certificate installation that has the specified `id`.

200.description: Certificate installation was found; details in response body.

post.description: Submits one or more certificate installation for TLS validation.

202.description: Certificate installations were submitted for validation.

post.summary: Request validation for a set of

post.description: Use this endpoint to retrieve certificate instance data according to specified search criteria based on commonly used field search parameters. Some examples are `signatureHashAlgorithm`, `validityEnd`, and `issuerCN`. For more information, see [common search parameters](https://docs.venafi.cloud/api/about-api-search-fields/#common-search-parameters)

200.description: Certificate Instances Response

post.summary: Retrieve certificate instance data matching search

get.description: Retrieves the details of all certificate requests.

200.description: The response body contains the details of the certificate requests

get.summary: Get the details of all certificate

post.description: Creates a certificate request for the specified `applicationId` and using the specified `certificateIssuingTemplateId`.  Indicate a renewal by specifying `existingCertificateId`. Set `isVaaSGenerated` to true, specify `csrAttributes` to request a certificate using *Automated Secure Keypair*. The `validityPeriod` value must be in the ISO8601 format for time duration. Examples of ISO8601 format time duration include _P1Y_ for one year, _P10D_ for ten days, and _PT12H_ for twelve hours.

201.description: A certificate request has been created, and its details can be found in the response body.

400.description: The request is either incomplete or malformed.

412.description: The request is either incomplete or malformed.

post.summary: Create a certificate request

get.description: Retrieves the details of the certificate request with the specified `id`.

200.description: A certificate request has been found, and its details are in the response body.

400.description: The request is either incomplete or malformed.

404.description: The certificate request was not found.

412.description: The request is either incomplete or malformed.

post.description: Resubmits the certificate request that has the specified `id`.

200.description: Certificate Request was resubmitted; details in response body.

post.summary: Resubmit a certificate request

post.description: This operation validates a proposed certificate request without actually submitting it.Checks for compliance with the specified issuing template and verifies all other references are valid (e.g., `existingCertificateId` is the UUID of an inventory certificate).

201.description: Certificate Request was validated.

post.summary: Validate a certificate request

post.description: Retrieves the details of certificate requests that match the specified [search expression](https://docs.venafi.cloud/CSH_api_search).

200.description: The response body contains the details of the certificate requests.

400.description: The request is either incomplete or malformed.

412.description: The request is either incomplete or malformed.

post.summary: Get the details of certificate requests

get.description: Retrieve updates configuration for tenant

200.description: Tenant updates configuration

requestBody.description: Modify updates configuration using patch behavior. All top level properties are optional

200.description: Updates configuration created/updated

put.description:
Can be used to update specific fields. Only fields specified in the request will be updated.
When the connector’s target is a webhook (`generic`, `slack`, or `teams`), updating the connector triggers the same outbound connectivity check as creation. The service performs a live POST request Certificate Manager - SaaS to the configured `connection.url`. If the endpoint is unreachable (for example, due to DNS failure, TLS handshake issues, or network/firewall restrictions), the update request fails with a descriptive error.

200.description: Connector Updated

delete.description:
Delete a Connector.
Deleting a connector removes its configuration for delivering events to the target endpoint, but it does not modify any customer firewall allowlisting or network settings. If your webhook endpoint requires allowlisting Venafi Cloud NAT IP addresses, that configuration must be managed separately.

get.description: Retrieve a list of all Connectors for a company

200.description: Success

get.summary: Retrieve all Connectors for a company

post.description: Create a Connector for your company. When the connector’s target is a webhook (`generic`, `slack`, or `teams`), the service performs a live outbound POST request from Certificate Manager - SaaS to the specified `connection.url` during creation. This validates DNS resolution, TLS handshake, and network reachability. If the URL is unreachable (for example, blocked by firewall rules or incorrect DNS), the connector creation request fails with a timeout or connection error. This behavior is expected and prevents creating connectors that cannot receive webhook notifications.

201.description: Connector Created

post.summary: Create a Connector

post.description: Adds a new Issuer Configuration, which links the following together - Sub CA Provider, Policies used to determine which certificates can be issued, and the IdP (Identity Provider) the Issuer should trust when receiving signed JWTs from its clients.

201.description: Issuer configuration successfully created; details in response body.

post.summary: Create a new Issuer configuration

get.description: Retrieves a list of all Issuer Configurations. Configurations link together Sub CA Providers, Workload Issuance Policies and Identity Provider information. Together these allow an instance of an Issuer to obtain a Sub CA certificate and use it to issue certificates.

200.description: All Issuer configurations.

get.summary: Get the details of all Issuer

get.description: Retrieves the details of the existing Issuer configuration for the specified `id`.

patch.description: Updates (replaces) fields on an Issuer configuration that has the specified `id`. Only fields specified in the request will be updated. Fields not specified in the request are not modified.

requestBody.description: Issuer configuration's details to update.

200.description: Specified Issuer configuration updated; details in response body.

404.description: Specified Issuer configuration was not found.

delete.description: Deletes the Issuer configuration for the specified `id`. Before deleting a configuration, be sure no Issuer instances use that configuration, or they will no longer function.

200.description: Specified Issuer configuration was deleted.

get.description: Retrieves all the Service Accounts the user has access to

200.description: The list of all Service Account details the user has access to

get.summary: Retrieves all the Service Accounts the

post.description: Creates a Service Account with the information provided. The service account is later used for machine to machine authentication. For rsaKey authentication service accounts publicKey key is required. Provide authenticationType field for creating other service accounts with another type of authentication.

requestBody.description: The Service Account data

200.description: The id assigned to the created Service Account. If the Service Account has CyberArk registry purpose and is created with ociToken authentication type, the ociRegistryToken and ociAccountName are returned in the response.

400.description: BadRequest. Return ErrInvalidPayload when creating rsaKey authentication service accounts and publicKey not provided.

post.summary: Creates a Service Account

get.description: Retrieves the information of an Service Account associated to the given id

parameters.description: The ID corresponding to the Service Account you want to retrieve
parameters.name: id

200.description: The Service Account details for the given id

patch.description: Updates the specified fields for a given service account matching the provided id. Providing either a new credentialLifetime or a new publicKey will result of the corresponding credentials associated to this Service Account

parameters.description: The ID corresponding to the Service Account you want to update
parameters.name: id

requestBody.description: The data to update

204.description: The Service Account was updated successfully

delete.description: Deletes the Service Account corresponding to the provided id. Deleting a Service Account also invalidates its associated credentials.

parameters.description: The ID corresponding to the Service Account you want to delete
parameters.name: id

204.description: The Service Account was deleted successfully

get.description: Retrieves all the Service Accounts Scopes available for the user

200.description: The list of all Service Account Scopes available for the user

get.summary: Retrieves all the Service Accounts Scopes

put.description: Regenerate the OCI registry token for a given service account matching the provided id. Providing a new OCI registry token to this Service Account and sending it back to the client back. Calling this endpoint will remove the current token for the service account and create a new one. Old token can not be used.

parameters.description: The ID corresponding to the Service Account you want to regenerate the OCI registry token
parameters.name: id

200.description: The Service Account OCI registry token has been regenerated

400.description: BadRequest. This also happens when trying to regenerate token for account without token based authentication

put.summary: Regenerate the OCI registry token for

put.description: Updates the specified credentials for a given service account matching the provided id. Providing either a new credentialLifetime or a new publicKey will result of the corresponding credentials associated to this Service Account

parameters.description: The ID corresponding to the Service Account you want to update
parameters.name: id

requestBody.description: The data to update

200.description: The Service Account Credentials were updated successfully

connection.description:
JSON object that configures the webhook connection for this target.
Supported fields:
- `url` (string, required): HTTPS endpoint URL for the webhook.
- `headers` (object, optional): Key/value pairs of HTTP headers. Only the `Authorization` header is honored by the webhook service; all other headers are ignored.
- `secret` (string, optional): Shared secret used to generate an HMAC signature sent in the `VaaS-Signature` header.
Webhook deliveries always use `Content-Type: application/json`. Custom `Content-Type` values are not supported.
Example:
 ```json
{
"url": "https://example.com/webhook",
"headers": {
"Authorization": "Splunk 12345678-ABCD"
},
"secret": "my-shared-secret"
 }

type.description:
The delivery mechanism for this connector. For webhook-based notifications, use:
- `generic` for a standard HTTPS webhook endpoint
- `slack` for a Slack webhook target
- `teams` for a Microsoft Teams webhook target

additionalRecipients.description: A list of users and/or teams who should be added as recipients.

channels.description: A list of channels that should be used to deliver the notification.

enabled.description: If true, then the system will send notification for certificates which are nearing expiration.

fallbackToPKIAdmin.description: If true, then all users with the PKI admin role will be added as recipients if the persona list does not include any recipients for a specific certificate.

includeCertificateDetails.description: If true, then the notification may include potentially sensitive details about the certificate that is nearing expiration.

personas.description: A list of personas who should be recipients of the notification. If "Application Owners" is included then the owners of the Application object to which the certificate is assigned will be added as recipients. If "All PKI Admins" or "All Admins" are present then all users in the system with that role will be added as recipients.

additionalRecipients.description: A list of users and/or teams who should be added as recipients.

channels.description: A list of channels that should be used to deliver the notification.

enabled.description: If true, then the system will send reports providing details of certificates which are nearing expiration.

expiringWithinDays.description: Any certificates which will expire within the number of days specified here will be included in the report.

ignoreAfterDays.description: Any certificates which have expired for more than the specified days will be excluded in the report.

roles.description: All users with the specified VaaS roles will be added as a report recipient.

schedule.description: A cron string representing the desired schedule for delivering reports. Note that currently reports may not be sent more than once per day. The `hour` and `minute` fields of the cron schedule will be ignored.

renewalActions.description: A set of options that can be used to specify the actions that should be taken when the auto-renewal process runs.

provision.description: If true, the system with attempt to provision any certificates associated with this application after a successful auto-renewal.

renew.description: If true, the system will attempt to renew any certificates associated with this application that are expected to expire within the configured number of days.

renewalWindow.description: A set of options to control the time window in which auto-renewal of certificates should be attempted.

days.description: Any certificates associated with this object that are expected to expire within the specified number of days will be acted on.

scmOAuth.description: Strata Cloud Manager APIs authenticate client requests using the OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials flow. Please use the `client_id`, `client_secret` values associated with an IAM service account along with a scope value of `tsg_id:XXXXXXXXXX`, where `XXXXXXXXXX` is the Tenant Service Group (TSG) ID. The resulting JWT access token should be attached to all API calls as a `Bearer` token in the `Authorization` header (ex. `Authorization: Bearer tokenstring`).

scmToken.description: Strata Cloud Manager APIs authenticate client requests using the OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials flow. Please use the `client_id`, `client_secret` values associated with an IAM service account along with a scope value of `tsg_id:XXXXXXXXXX`, where `XXXXXXXXXX` is the Tenant Service Group (TSG) ID. The resulting JWT access token should be attached to all API calls as a `Bearer` token in the `Authorization` header (ex. `Authorization: Bearer tokenstring`).
